intro: GridWatch is nonpartisan: it shows the process, not a position. Below are the real, public ways a Hoosier can weigh in on data-center and grid decisions.

items.title: Submit a comment on an IURC case
items.detail: The OUCC represents ratepayers and collects public comments on active IURC cases. Reference the Cause number (e.g., 46394 for Google/AES).
items.deadline: Ongoing; before each case's order date

items.title: Google / AES decision — Cause 46394
items.detail: The IURC's full order on the 1,200 MW Google Monrovia agreement is expected September 2026. This is the highest-attention case in the state.

items.title: Citizens Action Coalition
items.detail: The consumer-advocacy group intervening in IURC data-center cases. Sign up for alerts, or contact staff to get involved.

items.title: Indianapolis data-center moratorium
items.detail: Advocacy for a moratorium while Indianapolis writes data-center zoning rules (Martindale-Brightwood, Decatur Township).

items.title: Southwest Indiana — no data center
items.detail: Opposition to proposals in CenterPoint (SW Indiana) territory, incl. the Gibson/Posey proposal.

items.title: County plan-commission meetings
items.detail: Rezonings and variances for data centers are decided by county/city plan commissions and councils — usually monthly, open to public comment. Check your county's plan-commission calendar.

items.title: Read the filings yourself
items.detail: Every megawatt figure and cost claim on this map traces to a public filing. Search any Cause number to read the source documents (note: utilities frequently redact detail).
